elixir_tests.notes: Ran from backends/elixir-ingary; 1 property and 19 ExUnit tests passed after the session_run review fix.

gleam_tests.notes: Gleam core is compiled by mix_gleam and covered by test/gleam_history_core_test.exs.

python_oracles.notes: Per instruction, no uv, pytest, hidden evaluator, or Python bakeoff tests were run.

elixir_runtime_shell: Plug/Cowboy routing and test/admin HTTP APIs
application supervision, dynamic model/session registries, and DynamicSupervisors
session GenServer ownership for bounded event history
boundary_shape.ffi_or_interop: Elixir calls the generated Erlang module :elixir_ingary@history_core directly. Regex and literal predicate evaluation remains in Elixir because Gleam regex interop would have required additional Erlang FFI for this spike.

isolation_behavior.model_runtime: Synthetic model/version runtimes are represented by Registry-backed GenServers under ElixirIngary.ModelSupervisor.
isolation_behavior.session_runtime: Session/run history is owned by Registry-backed GenServers under ElixirIngary.SessionSupervisor.
isolation_behavior.tested: A test crashes one session worker and verifies another session under the same model retains history; it also starts another model runtime.
isolation_behavior.limitation: The session supervisor is global rather than physically nested below each model runtime. The registry key still scopes by model/version/session/run, but model-subtree teardown semantics are modeled rather than fully hierarchical.

sidecar_backpressure_risks.queue_growth: Each session GenServer serializes writes for that session. A hot session can queue its own history calls but should not serialize unrelated sessions.
sidecar_backpressure_risks.restart_storms: A repeatedly crashing session can restart under the DynamicSupervisor; there is no explicit restart-rate telemetry or circuit breaker in this prototype.
sidecar_backpressure_risks.cold_start_latency: First event or read for a session starts a GenServer; observed in tests as local in-process startup only.
sidecar_backpressure_risks.protocol_failures: No external protocol boundary is used.
sidecar_backpressure_risks.unrelated_slowdown: Unrelated sessions use distinct GenServers; they still share BEAM schedulers and the global DynamicSupervisor.

limitations: Gleam owns the count/decision classification but not event decoding, regex compilation, or receipt assembly.
Session cache state is in-memory only and intentionally resets on test config replacement or session crash.
The model runtime hierarchy is not a full per-model nested supervisor tree.
No load/p95/p99 latency benchmark was added inside the timebox.
Invalid regex currently behaves as a non-match rather than returning a configuration error.

mutation_check_self_review.performed: Reasoned mutation review after test design: changing threshold comparison from >= to > would fail threshold trigger tests; removing scope filtering would fail out-of-session tests; reversing eviction sort would fail the property test; skipping chat history ingestion would fail the normal chat test.
mutation_check_self_review.not_performed: No automated mutation-testing tool was run.

setup_friction: mise initially refused the repo config until mise trust was run after inspecting mise.toml.
Gleam was not pinned initially; the root mise.toml now pins gleam 1.16.0.
mix_gleam required erlc_paths, erlc_include_path, prune_code_paths: false, and a deps.get alias so generated Erlang modules are compiled and callable from Elixir.
gleam_stdlib had to be pinned consistently between mix.lock and Gleam manifest.
